content.text:
{"success":true,"process_rule_id":"J09B","process_rule":"1. Copy all rows from the decision table into a temporary table `J09B_temp`, but only those rows where the tour identifier starts with `J09B`.

2. Add a new text column `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to table `J09B_temp`.

3. Classify meter reading type and set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` in `J09B_temp`:
   - For each `HAUS` in `J09B_temp`, count how many distinct `EQUNR` are associated with that `HAUS`.
   - If a `HAUS` has less than 3 `EQUNR`, set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to `"KSA"` for all rows of that `HAUS`.
   - Else set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to `"EVU"` for all rows of that `HAUS`.
   - (Rule from annotation: `1. Set ENTSCHEIDUNG to "KSA" when a HAUS has less than 3 EQUNR. ELSE set ENTSCHEIDUNG to "EVU"`.)

4. Join `J09B_temp` with the `EABLG` table using the `INSTALLATION` column, extending `J09B_temp` with the matching `EABLG` data.

5. Join the resulting `J09B_temp` with both `NET_MASTER` and `SALES_MASTER` using the `EQUNR` column as join key, extending `J09B_temp` with the corresponding customer master data.
   - (From annotation: `JOIN table with NET_MASTER and SALES_MASTER table using EQUNR`.)

6. Compare, for each row in `J09B_temp`, the customer data coming from `SALES_MASTER` (sales customer data) and from `NET_MASTER` (net data customer) to determine which customer data is available for that row.

7. For each row in `J09B_temp`, decide which customer data to use based on the comparison:
   - If the sales customer data (from `SALES_MASTER`) for that row is NOT empty:
     7.1. Use the sales customer data table entries as the customer data for that row (e.g., name, address, email, and related customer fields in `J09B_temp` are taken from `SALES_MASTER`).
   - Else:
     7.2. Use the net data customer table entries as the customer data for that row (e.g., name, address, email, and related customer fields in `J09B_temp` are taken from `NET_MASTER`).

8. Export the final contents of `J09B_temp` as a CSV file named `J09B` (e.g., `J09B.csv`), using exactly the following column names in the export:
   - `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`
   - `HAUS`
   - `ANLAGE`
   - `ME_MA_ID`
   - `EQUNR`
   - `HOUSE_NUM1`
   - `ENTSCHEIDUNG`
   - `TITLE`
   - `FORENAME`
   - `SURNAME`
   - `STREET`
   - `STREETNO`
   - `POST_CODE1`
   - `CITY1`
   - `CITY2`
   - `EMAIL`  
   (From annotation: `Use this column names, when exporting the file:
   `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`, `HAUS`, `ANLAGE`, `ME_MA_ID`, `EQUNR`, `HOUSE_NUM1`, `ENTSCHEIDUNG`, `TITLE`, `FORENAME`, `SURNAME`, `STREET`, `STREETNO`, `POST_CODE1`, `CITY1`, `CITY2`, `EMAIL``.)

9. Send an email to the EVU with the exported CSV file attached:
   - Recipient email address: `meter.readings@evu.com`
   - Attach the CSV file from: `file_path: <path_to_exported_csv_file>`
   - (From annotation: `Email address meter.readings@evu.com
     file_path: <path_to_exported_csv_file>`.)

10. After the email has been successfully sent, delete the temporary table `J09B_temp` (and any other temporary artifacts created solely for this process) to clean up."}

structured_content.process_rule:
1. Copy all rows from the decision table into a temporary table `J09B_temp`, but only those rows where the tour identifier starts with `J09B`.

2. Add a new text column `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to table `J09B_temp`.

3. Classify meter reading type and set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` in `J09B_temp`:
   - For each `HAUS` in `J09B_temp`, count how many distinct `EQUNR` are associated with that `HAUS`.
   - If a `HAUS` has less than 3 `EQUNR`, set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to `"KSA"` for all rows of that `HAUS`.
   - Else set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to `"EVU"` for all rows of that `HAUS`.
   - (Rule from annotation: `1. Set ENTSCHEIDUNG to "KSA" when a HAUS has less than 3 EQUNR. ELSE set ENTSCHEIDUNG to "EVU"`.)

4. Join `J09B_temp` with the `EABLG` table using the `INSTALLATION` column, extending `J09B_temp` with the matching `EABLG` data.

5. Join the resulting `J09B_temp` with both `NET_MASTER` and `SALES_MASTER` using the `EQUNR` column as join key, extending `J09B_temp` with the corresponding customer master data.
   - (From annotation: `JOIN table with NET_MASTER and SALES_MASTER table using EQUNR`.)

6. Compare, for each row in `J09B_temp`, the customer data coming from `SALES_MASTER` (sales customer data) and from `NET_MASTER` (net data customer) to determine which customer data is available for that row.

7. For each row in `J09B_temp`, decide which customer data to use based on the comparison:
   - If the sales customer data (from `SALES_MASTER`) for that row is NOT empty:
     7.1. Use the sales customer data table entries as the customer data for that row (e.g., name, address, email, and related customer fields in `J09B_temp` are taken from `SALES_MASTER`).
   - Else:
     7.2. Use the net data customer table entries as the customer data for that row (e.g., name, address, email, and related customer fields in `J09B_temp` are taken from `NET_MASTER`).

8. Export the final contents of `J09B_temp` as a CSV file named `J09B` (e.g., `J09B.csv`), using exactly the following column names in the export:
   - `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`
   - `HAUS`
   - `ANLAGE`
   - `ME_MA_ID`
   - `EQUNR`
   - `HOUSE_NUM1`
   - `ENTSCHEIDUNG`
   - `TITLE`
   - `FORENAME`
   - `SURNAME`
   - `STREET`
   - `STREETNO`
   - `POST_CODE1`
   - `CITY1`
   - `CITY2`
   - `EMAIL`  
   (From annotation: `Use this column names, when exporting the file:
   `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`, `HAUS`, `ANLAGE`, `ME_MA_ID`, `EQUNR`, `HOUSE_NUM1`, `ENTSCHEIDUNG`, `TITLE`, `FORENAME`, `SURNAME`, `STREET`, `STREETNO`, `POST_CODE1`, `CITY1`, `CITY2`, `EMAIL``.)

9. Send an email to the EVU with the exported CSV file attached:
   - Recipient email address: `meter.readings@evu.com`
   - Attach the CSV file from: `file_path: <path_to_exported_csv_file>`
   - (From annotation: `Email address meter.readings@evu.com
     file_path: <path_to_exported_csv_file>`.)

10. After the email has been successfully sent, delete the temporary table `J09B_temp` (and any other temporary artifacts created solely for this process) to clean up.

args.sqlite_command:
CREATE TABLE J09B_temp AS
SELECT d.*, NULL AS ENTSCHEIDUNG
FROM decision_talbe d
WHERE d.TOUR LIKE 'J09B%';

WITH haus_counts AS (
    SELECT HAUS, COUNT(DISTINCT EQUNR) AS eq_count
    FROM J09B_temp
    GROUP BY HAUS
)
UPDATE J09B_temp
SET ENTSCHEIDUNG = CASE
    WHEN hc.eq_count < 3 THEN 'KSA'
    ELSE 'EVU'
END
FROM haus_counts hc
WHERE J09B_temp.HAUS = hc.HAUS;

CREATE TABLE J09B_temp2 AS
SELECT jt.*, eablg."Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.", eablg."Meter Reading reason", eablg."MR Reason - Text", eablg."Scheduled MR Date", eablg."Meter Reading unit", eablg."Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No..1"
FROM J09B_temp jt
LEFT JOIN eablg ON jt.ANLAGE = eablg.Installation;

CREATE TABLE J09B_temp3 AS
SELECT t2.*, 
       sm.TITLE AS S_TITLE, sm.FORENAME AS S_FORENAME, sm.SURNAME AS S_SURNAME,
       sm.STREET AS S_STREET, sm.STREETNO AS S_STREETNO, sm.POST_CODE1 AS S_POST_CODE1,
       sm.CITY1 AS S_CITY1, sm.CITY2 AS S_CITY2, sm.EMAIL AS S_EMAIL,
       nm.TITLE AS N_TITLE, nm.FORENAME AS N_FORENAME, nm.SURNAME AS N_SURNAME,
       nm.STREET AS N_STREET, nm.STREETNO AS N_STREETNO, nm.POST_CODE1 AS N_POST_CODE1,
       nm.CITY1 AS N_CITY1, nm.CITY2 AS N_CITY2, nm.EMAIL AS N_EMAIL
FROM J09B_temp2 t2
LEFT JOIN sales_master sm ON t2.EQUNR = sm.EQUNR
LEFT JOIN net_master nm ON t2.EQUNR = nm.EQUNR;

CREATE TABLE J09B_final AS
SELECT 
  "Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.",
  HAUS,
  ANLAGE,
  ME_MA_ID,
  EQUNR,
  HOUSE_NUM1,
  ENTSCHEIDUNG,
  CASE WHEN S_TITLE IS NOT NULL AND S_TITLE <> '' THEN S_TITLE ELSE N_TITLE END AS TITLE,
  CASE WHEN S_FORENAME IS NOT NULL AND S_FORENAME <> '' THEN S_FORENAME ELSE N_FORENAME END AS FORENAME,
  CASE WHEN S_SURNAME IS NOT NULL AND S_SURNAME <> '' THEN S_SURNAME ELSE N_SURNAME END AS SURNAME,
  CASE WHEN S_STREET IS NOT NULL AND S_STREET <> '' THEN S_STREET ELSE N_STREET END AS STREET,
  CASE WHEN S_STREETNO IS NOT NULL AND S_STREETNO <> '' THEN S_STREETNO ELSE N_STREETNO END AS STREETNO,
  CASE WHEN S_POST_CODE1 IS NOT NULL THEN S_POST_CODE1 ELSE N_POST_CODE1 END AS POST_CODE1,
  CASE WHEN S_CITY1 IS NOT NULL AND S_CITY1 <> '' THEN S_CITY1 ELSE N_CITY1 END AS CITY1,
  CASE WHEN S_CITY2 IS NOT NULL AND S_CITY2 <> '' THEN S_CITY2 ELSE N_CITY2 END AS CITY2,
  CASE WHEN S_EMAIL IS NOT NULL AND S_EMAIL <> '' THEN S_EMAIL ELSE N_EMAIL END AS EMAIL
FROM J09B_temp3;

args.sql_query:
SELECT 
  "Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.",
  HAUS,
  ANLAGE,
  ME_MA_ID,
  EQUNR,
  HOUSE_NUM1,
  ENTSCHEIDUNG,
  TITLE,
  FORENAME,
  SURNAME,
  STREET,
  STREETNO,
  POST_CODE1,
  CITY1,
  CITY2,
  EMAIL
FROM J09B_final;

args.email:
Dear EVU Team,

Please find attached the meter reading file for tour J09B.

Best regards,
Meter Reading Automation System

kwargs.content:
The process for `process_rule_id 'J09B'` has been executed end‑to‑end:

- All relevant `decision_talbe` rows with `TOUR` starting `J09B` were copied into `J09B_temp`.
- `ENTSCHEIDUNG` was set per `HAUS`: `KSA` if the house has fewer than 3 distinct `EQUNR`, otherwise `EVU`.
- The data was joined with `EABLG` (via installation) and then with `NET_MASTER` and `SALES_MASTER` (via `EQUNR`).
- For each row, available `SALES_MASTER` data was preferred; if missing, `NET_MASTER` data was used.
- A CSV export `J09B.csv` was created with the required columns and 20,180 rows.
- An email with `J09B.csv` attached was sent to `meter.readings@evu.com`.
- All temporary tables (`J09B_temp`, `J09B_temp2`, `J09B_temp3`, `J09B_final`) were dropped.
